name: Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur (France)

colors.symbolism: the gold (or) field of the historic arms of Provence
the four red (gules) pallets of the arms of Provence and the gules of the County of Nice
the azure field of the arms of Dauphine

description: A composite armorial banner combining the historic heraldry of Provence (gold with four red pallets), Dauphine (blue with a gold dolphin), and the County of Nice.
history: Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur (PACA) is a French region whose territory covers the historic regions of Provence, the southern Dauphine, and the County of Nice. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite armorial banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: A composite armorial banner combining the historic arms of the constituent provinces - Provence (gold with red pallets), Dauphine (blue with gold dolphin), and the County of Nice.

features.role: the combined arms of Provence (or, four pallets gules), Dauphine (azure, a dolphin or), and the County of Nice
features.symbolism: Each quarter of the banner displays the historic arms of one of the provinces brought together in the modern PACA region: Provence with its four red pallets on gold, Dauphine with its gold dolphin on azure, and the County of Nice.
